task.title: Character memory continuity and portable Memory Recall imports
task.source: GitHub issue #1047 plus reported Memory Recall import failure
task.classification: cross-boundary storage, import/export, prompt, and UI change

coreClaim: Explicitly attributed character memories persist and recall across Conversations and Roleplays by default while chat-only and ambiguous captures remain local, and Memory Recall files import without resolving destination provider connections.
rootCause: Automatic capture refreshed only chat-local memory rows, canonical recall required an opt-in metadata flag, and file import tried to validate and use the destination embedding connection instead of treating imported memory content as portable data.

inScope: Character-level persistence preference with a legacy-safe default
Automatic canonical character-memory upsert and cross-mode recall
Character memory management and inherited chat-memory display
Character duplicate and delete lifecycle handling
outOfScope: Automatically promoting historical chat memories into character scope
Changing memory summarization quality or model selection
Provider transport, secrets, or connection CRUD

claimBoundary.coreClaim: New explicitly attributed memories follow a character across Conversation and Roleplay by default; explicit chat-only, ambiguous, legacy-job, and disabled-recall paths do not; imported files rebuild local lexical vectors without provider resolution.
claimBoundary.riskType: cross-entrypoint storage compatibility, import/export, prompt assembly, and destructive cleanup
entrypoints: Saved assistant generation completion in Conversation and Roleplay
Canonical-memory prompt assembly for later chats
Memory Recall JSON and .marinara file import
currentPathsOrFormats: Top-level character.memoryPersistence with character or chat values
Version 2 memory-capture-jobs with scopeKind, scopeId, scopeReason, characterId, and sceneId
canonical-memories rows scoped by character and memory-index-rows lexical indexes
Memory Recall import envelopes containing chunks or memories
Character memory export envelope de_koi_character_memories version 1
legacyPathsOrFormats: Characters with no memoryPersistence field resolve to character-wide continuity
Version 1 or unscoped memory-capture jobs remain chat-local
Existing chat-only memory rows remain in their original chat until explicitly copied
Imported memory chunks carrying stale vectors or provider metadata discard those fields and rebuild lexical vectors
outOfScopeClaims: The PR does not migrate all historical local chat memories automatically.
The PR does not claim a live browser visual pass.
The PR does not alter provider request transport, secrets, or connection records.
The PR does not make ambiguous group narration a character memory.

contractLaneGate.brokenContract: src/engine automatic capture and prompt assembly treated chat-local refresh as the final memory owner, while src-tauri import mechanics treated destination provider configuration as part of a portable file's validity.
contractLaneGate.producer: src/engine/generation/automatic-memory-capture-queue.ts for scoped memory facts and src-tauri/src/commands/storage/chat_memory.rs for imported chat-memory rows
contractLaneGate.consumer: src/engine/generation/canonical-memory-context.ts, character Memories UI, chat Memory Console, and future prompt assembly
contractLaneGate.impliedContract: src/engine owns whether a captured fact follows a character or stays local; src-tauri owns durable storage and deterministic import mechanics; src/features only renders and invokes those contracts.
contractLaneGate.actualEnforcement: The queue persists a resolved scope, upserts one stable canonical character row after local capture, canonical recall filters active across-enabled characters, and Rust import strips provider/vector fields before local lexical indexing.
contractLaneGate.primaryOwnerDetail: src/engine owns character-memory scope and recall semantics; src-tauri owns portable import and deletion mechanics; src/features consumes both through existing shared API wrappers.
contractLaneGate.wrongLaneFixToAvoid: Do not decide memory scope in React, copy canonical-memory formulas into UI, bypass focused shared APIs, or move portable JSON semantics into provider transport. Rust handles privileged persistence mechanics, not the product decision about who remembers.
regressionProof: automatic-memory-capture-queue.spec.ts proves default, opt-out, ambiguous, idempotent, legacy-job, and Conversation-to-Roleplay rows
canonical-memory-context.spec.ts proves default query, explicit disables, opt-out, and combined prompt copy
chat_memory.rs tests prove missing, invalid, and overridden provider connections cannot block import
entities.rs and characters.rs tests prove target-only cleanup and duplicate default behavior
character-memory-model.spec.ts and MemoryRecallMemoriesModal.spec.ts prove portable envelopes and read-only ownership display

positiveRows.claim: An attributed Conversation capture becomes one stable character memory and is recalled in a later Roleplay.
positiveRows.evidence: src/engine/generation/automatic-memory-capture-queue.spec.ts passes the canonical upsert, idempotent retry, and Conversation-to-Roleplay seam tests.

positiveRows.claim: A Memory Recall file imports even when the destination connection is missing, invalid, or lacks an embedding model.
positiveRows.evidence: src-tauri chat_memory import tests pass for missing targets, invalid chat overrides, stale file vectors, and deterministic lexical indexing.

positiveRows.claim: Users can manage character memories and inspect inherited rows without mutating them from the chat console.
positiveRows.evidence: character-memory-model.spec.ts, MemoryRecallMemoriesModal.spec.ts, ui.store.test.ts, import-export-icon-contract.spec.ts, typecheck, architecture check, and production build pass.

positiveRows.claim: The final repository baseline remains green.
positiveRows.evidence: pnpm test passes 375 files and 1,772 tests; pnpm check, pnpm build, pnpm check:architecture, cargo check --workspace, and git diff --check pass.

contradictionRows.claim: An explicit This chat only preference must not create or query a character memory.
contradictionRows.evidence: character-memory-scope.spec.ts, automatic-memory-capture-queue.spec.ts, and canonical-memory-context.spec.ts pass negative opt-out rows.

contradictionRows.claim: Ambiguous roleplay narration must not be assigned to an arbitrary character.
contradictionRows.evidence: automatic-memory-capture-queue.spec.ts proves unattributed roleplay capture stays scene-scoped and creates no canonical character row.

contradictionRows.claim: An imported file must not retain or invoke file-carried provider/vector metadata.
contradictionRows.evidence: chat_memory.rs import tests assert stale vectors and embeddingConnectionId/embeddingModel fields are discarded and replaced by equal lexical vectors.

contradictionRows.claim: Deleting one character must not delete another character's memories or indexes.

legacyDefaultRows.claim: Existing characters without a persistence field receive the new character-wide default.
legacyDefaultRows.evidence: character.schema.spec.ts and character-memory-scope.spec.ts pass missing/invalid legacy value rows.

legacyDefaultRows.claim: Existing unscoped queue jobs are not retroactively promoted into character scope.
legacyDefaultRows.evidence: automatic-memory-capture-queue.spec.ts passes the legacy job remains chat-local row.

legacyDefaultRows.claim: Duplicated characters receive a new identity and the default character-wide preference rather than copying an opt-out.
legacyDefaultRows.evidence: duplicate_character_copies_managed_avatar_file passes with a chat-only source and character-wide duplicate.

untestedRows: No dedicated screenshot was captured; focused UI helper contracts, the production build, and the hosted browser smoke and performance suite cover the changed UI path.
Historical local chat memories are not automatically promoted; the character Memories tab provides explicit row selection and copy.

ownedFacts.description: A missing character.memoryPersistence value resolves to character, while only the literal chat value opts out.
ownedFacts.evidence: effectiveCharacterMemoryPersistence tests cover undefined, invalid legacy, and chat values.

ownedFacts.description: Automatic canonical memory IDs derive deterministically from the versioned capture job ID.
ownedFacts.evidence: canonicalMemoryIdForJob returns canonical-${jobId}; the retry test proves only one stored row remains.

ownedFacts.description: Portable chat-memory import performs no destination provider lookup and stores deterministic lexical vectors.
ownedFacts.evidence: import_chat_memories no longer calls memory_embedding_context; three Rust tests pass with broken destination configuration.

userActionCopy.description: Before deleting a character whose learned history must be retained, export that character's memory JSON to a user-controlled location.
userActionCopy.destination: A user-selected folder outside De-Koi's application data directory
userActionCopy.action: Click Export in the Memories tab and copy or save the resulting JSON file to the destination before confirming character deletion.
userActionCopy.companionFiles: No provider connection, vector index, or companion file is required; those fields are intentionally excluded and rebuilt on import.
detectedLayouts: Current character-scoped canonical-memory rows export through de_koi_character_memories version 1.
Legacy chat-local memories are not included unless the user explicitly copies selected rows into the character first.

reviewThreadLedger.finding: Bunny boundary check found trailing whitespace and extra EOF blank lines.
reviewThreadLedger.fix: Removed the whitespace defects and reran git diff --check successfully.

reviewThreadLedger.finding: Bunny found reversed import/export arrows in the new character Memories tab.
reviewThreadLedger.fix: Applied the existing incoming Download/outgoing Upload contract and extended the narrow icon-direction test.

reviewThreadLedger.finding: Bunny found that Open character memories opened the Metadata tab.
reviewThreadLedger.fix: Added a one-shot character editor destination with a focused store test and routed the action to Memories.

reviewThreadLedger.finding: Ready-state Bunny found that malformed character-memory records were silently skipped.
reviewThreadLedger.fix: Made character-memory import reject malformed records atomically and display a destructive alert dialog instead of reporting partial success.

reviewThreadLedger.finding: Ready-state Bunny questioned whether repeated imports preserved tag and payload patches.
reviewThreadLedger.fix: Routed existing-record updates through an explicit typed patch builder and added a regression test proving tags and portable payload roots are preserved while import-only identity timestamps are excluded.

reviewThreadLedger.finding: Ready-state Bunny found inherited character memories lacked an explicit read-only cue.
reviewThreadLedger.fix: Added a visible Memory Console notice that inherited character memories are read-only and links editing to the character owner.

reviewThreadLedger.finding: Ready-state Bunny found export and clear scope was not explicit when inherited memories were visible.
reviewThreadLedger.fix: The same visible notice now states that export and clear affect only memories local to the current chat or scene.

verification.baselineEvidence: The full 375-file, 1,772-test suite passed with four bounded workers and full pnpm check passed after the ready-state Bunny repairs.
edgeRowsTested: Conversation capture recalled in later Roleplay
Import with missing, invalid, and incomplete embedding targets
Inherited read-only row and exact Memories deep link
negativeRowsTested: No canonical row for chat-only or ambiguous capture
No character-scope query when disabled or chat-only
No provider metadata retained during import

notes: Bunny local review passed after two in-scope findings were fixed.
Ready-state Bunny repairs now reject malformed imports atomically, preserve explicit repeat-import patches, and explain inherited/local memory scope in the UI.
The user explicitly authorized marking PR #1052 ready and merging it after all hosted gates pass.
